name: Open-Instruct
tagline: AllenAI's open codebase for instruction-tuning and post-training LLMs
metaDescription: Open-Instruct is AllenAI's open codebase for instruction-tuning and post-training LLMs with SFT, DPO and RLVR — the recipes behind Tulu and OLMo.
overview: Open-Instruct is AllenAI's open codebase for instruction-tuning and post-training pretrained language models on publicly available datasets. It collects the scripts, data formats, and training recipes the team used to build the Tulu and OLMo model families, so you can reproduce their work or apply the same methods to your own base models.

It covers the main stages of modern post-training: supervised finetuning (SFT) on instruction data, preference tuning with DPO, and reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR). The repo also ships reward-model training and a unified format for mixing instruction datasets, plus LoRA and QLoRA for parameter-efficient finetuning.

It is aimed at researchers and ML engineers who want a documented, reproducible pipeline rather than a one-line wrapper. As a fine-tuning framework, it sits alongside the released model checkpoints and the TÜLU 3 paper, giving you the full recipe from base model to instruction-following model.
features: Supervised finetuning (SFT) of pretrained models on instruction datasets in a unified format
Preference tuning with DPO and other preference-finetuning methods
Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR), plus reward-model (RM) training
Ready-made recipes for the Tulu 3 and OLMo 2 model families (Llama 3.1 8B/70B, OLMo-2 7B/13B)
LoRA and QLoRA support for parameter-efficient finetuning
Released checkpoints on HuggingFace for SFT, DPO, RLVR and reward-model stages

gettingStarted.intro: Open-Instruct uses uv for dependency management and ships ready-to-run training scripts. Clone the repo, sync dependencies, then launch one of the recipe scripts.

steps.heading: Install dependencies
steps.body: The repository uses uv for installation and running code. From the cloned repo, sync the environment.

steps.heading: Run supervised finetuning (SFT)
steps.body: Launch the Tulu 3 8B SFT recipe to instruction-tune a base model.

steps.heading: Run preference tuning (DPO)
steps.body: After SFT, run the DPO recipe to align the model on preference data.

steps.heading: Try the released models
steps.body: If you just want to use the results, download the Tulu and OLMo checkpoints from HuggingFace (for example allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B) or try the free demo at playground.allenai.org.

useCases: Reproduce the Tulu 3 or OLMo 2 post-training pipeline from base model to instruction-following model
Instruction-tune your own base model with SFT, DPO and RLVR using documented recipes
Train a reward model and run RLVR experiments on verifiable-reward tasks
Run parameter-efficient finetuning with LoRA or QLoRA when GPU memory is limited
